title: eBGP Peering Won't Establish With New Transit Provider

ticket.summary: The new eBGP session to transit provider NorthStar Networks was supposed to go live this morning but has never come up. edge-rtr01 shows the peering session stuck in Active/Idle and no prefixes are being learned from the new provider, so outbound traffic is still all going out the existing single ISP link.

topology.description: edge-rtr01 (AS 65010, our enterprise AS) has an existing eBGP session with our primary ISP and is being configured for a second eBGP session directly to NorthStar Networks' edge router ns-peer-edge04 (AS 64500) over a newly provisioned cross-connect at 198.51.100.0/30. Both sides exchanged the peering LOA (Letter of Authorization) which lists the AS numbers and IPs to use.

logs.content:
Jul 14 09:10:02 edge-rtr01 %BGP-5-ADJCHANGE: neighbor 198.51.100.2 Down BGP Notification sent
Jul 14 09:10:02 edge-rtr01 %BGP-3-NOTIFICATION: sent to neighbor 198.51.100.2 2/2 (peer in wrong AS) 2 bytes FDE4
Jul 14 09:10:05 edge-rtr01 %BGP-5-ADJCHANGE: neighbor 198.51.100.2 Up-to-Down state change, reason: Peer AS mismatch

Subject: RE: Peering turn-up - AS64500 <-> AS65010
Hi team, our edge (ns-peer-edge04) is configured and sees TCP connections arriving from your side on port 179 but the session resets almost immediately with an OPEN message error. Can you confirm the remote-as value you have configured for us? Our AS is 64500.

cli_outputs.output:
BGP router identifier 198.51.100.1, local AS number 65010
Neighbor        V    AS  MsgRcvd  MsgSent   TblVer  InQ OutQ Up/Down  State/PfxRcd
198.51.100.2    4  64599        3        3        1    0    0 00:00:12 Active

router bgp 65010
 neighbor 198.51.100.2 remote-as 64599
 neighbor 198.51.100.2 description NorthStar-Networks-Transit
 neighbor 198.51.100.2 password ********

BGP neighbor is 198.51.100.1, remote AS 65010, external link
  BGP state = Idle, Last read never, Last write never
  Notification: received 2/2 (peer in wrong AS)
  Configured local AS is 64500

monitoring.notes: The peering dashboard shows the session to 198.51.100.2 flapping between Idle and Active every ~30-60 seconds since the session was first configured at 09:05, never reaching Established. No prefixes have ever been received on this session (PfxRcd has stayed at 0 the entire time), consistent with a session-establishment failure rather than a route-policy or filtering issue.

hints: The session never reaches Established and BGP notification code 2/2 is being sent — that specific error code means 'peer in wrong AS', an OPEN message error.
Compare the remote-as configured on edge-rtr01 for neighbor 198.51.100.2 against NorthStar's actual AS number from the LOA.
edge-rtr01 has 'neighbor 198.51.100.2 remote-as 64599' configured, but NorthStar's edge router ns-peer-edge04 is actually in AS 64500, not 64599 — a typo'd remote AS number.

solution.root_cause: edge-rtr01 was configured with 'neighbor 198.51.100.2 remote-as 64599', a typo of NorthStar Networks' actual AS number, 64500. BGP OPEN messages include the sender's AS, and when it doesn't match the configured remote-as, the session is rejected with notification code 2/2 (OPEN Message Error, peer in wrong AS), so the session stays in Active/Idle and never reaches Established.
solution.fix: Correct the neighbor statement on edge-rtr01 to 'neighbor 198.51.100.2 remote-as 64500' to match NorthStar's actual AS number from the peering LOA.
